stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • The arctic skua (Stercorarius parasiticus) is a seabird that feeds mainly on fish.
  • It is unusual among seabirds in that it rarely dives below the surface to catch fish on its own.
  • One way it obtains food is by chasing other birds in flight until they drop or regurgitate their catch.
  • Another way it obtains food is by skimming the surface and snatching small fish driven up by feeding whales.
stem: The student wants to emphasize a similarity between the two ways an arctic skua obtains food. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: An arctic skua rarely dives below the surface, instead chasing other birds until they drop their catch.
Of the arctic skua's two ways of obtaining food, only one is known as kleptoparasitism.
In addition to chasing other birds for their catch, an arctic skua snatches small fish from the surface.
Neither of the arctic skua's two ways of obtaining food requires it to dive below the surface.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer because it identifies what the two methods share: neither requires diving.

Choices A and C describe one or both methods but do not highlight a similarity. Choice B emphasizes a difference, not a similarity.
